summary: @wagni_bot/jupiter-sdk postinstall harvests host/user/filesystem data over HTTP
details: Version 1.0.0 of this package ships a postinstall.js script that executes automatically on npm install. The script imports fs, os, and http, and reads host-identifying information via os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and os.homedir(), then performs filesystem enumeration under the user's home directory via multiple fs.existsSync() probes. The combination of home-directory reconnaissance, host/user identity collection, and plain HTTP outbound in an install-time lifecycle hook is the credential/host-recon exfiltration shape and matches no legitimate purpose for a package that presents itself as a Jupiter SDK. Automated tracing of the file content was withheld by the model safety filter, consistent with operational malware content. Installing the package causes the installer's host identity and home-directory contents/paths to be transmitted to an author-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
